description: private, day & boarding, college-prep school in Tacoma, Washington, United States
description_wiki: Annie Wright Schools is a private school in Tacoma, Washington, United States.

description: county seat of Pierce County, Washington, United States
description_wiki: Tacoma ( tə-KOH-mə) is a city in and the county seat of Pierce County, Washington, United States.
Tacoma Opera is the Pacific Northwest's second-largest professional opera company located in Tacoma, WA and is a member of OPERA America.

description: comic opera by Gilbert and Sullivan
description_wiki: Iolanthe; or, The Peer and the Peri () is a comic opera with music by Arthur Sullivan and libretto by W. S. Gilbert, first performed in 1882.

aliases: W. S. Gilbert and Arthur Sullivan
description_wiki: Gilbert and Sullivan were a Victorian-era theatrical partnership of the dramatist W. S. Gilbert and composer Arthur Sullivan.

description: cultural and religious celebration on 17 March
description_wiki: Saint Patrick's Day, or the Feast of Saint Patrick (Irish: Lá Fhéile Pádraig, lit. 'the Day of the Festival of Patrick'), is a religious and cultural holiday held on 17 March, the traditional death date of Saint Patrick (c. 385 – c. 461), the foremost patron saint of Ireland.

The global COVID-19 pandemic (also known as the coronavirus pandemic), caused by severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus 2 (SARS-CoV-2), began with an outbreak in Wuhan, China, in December 2019.

description: United States government public health agency
description_wiki: The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) is the national public health agency of the United States.

description: city in and county seat of King County, State of Washington, United States
description_wiki: Seattle (  see-AT-əl) is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Washington and the Pacific Northwest region of North America.
